## [00:00 - 03:04]

In this video, I'm going to show you how to set up an account really, really quickly, really, really easily based on the templates that I use for e-commerce. So this spreadsheet here, which you have access to, has the campaign structure as I would set up a new account. And it means that using this kind of structure, you have it already in your account ready to use and you use it when you need to bring new accounts in. So let me just show you how using the simple import feature within Facebook, you can take this data here, upload it and have the account structure ready to use. So this data here, these three columns are the only three columns you really need to set up an account. So what I'm going to do is copy these and paste those into Facebook. So here I'm going to select the import feature. So now as I've copied the data from the spreadsheet, I can now import the ads in bulk, but choosing this option to paste text from a tab separated document, which is what's created when you copy multiple sales across a table, for example, in Google Sheets. So I'm just going to click that and paste that in. And when I hit import, Facebook is going to translate that into the core data it needs to create a campaign. So here you can see when I close this. I have my campaign all set up. I'll just show it by order of campaign. And essentially what I have here is each of the campaigns, the campaigns that I would be looking at to run my preferred template for running an e-commerce account. So you have your cold, your hot and your warm audiences and also existing. Now that I've done that, I can then update Facebook with the new data I've sent through. But here's one thing if you've noticed in the notes, in order to create a product catalog, you need a catalog already set up. So with the rest of these objectives, once you upload these into Facebook, that's it. They're done. They're ready now for you to put adsets in. But for the product catalog, you actually need a product catalog. So if I hit confirm here, we should see one error come up for the product catalog. And here it is here. So some items not confirmed. And what it's saying here is that you need to have essentially the product catalog for that one. So all of my other campaigns have successfully uploaded. All I need to do is click edit, select a catalog. I've just chosen the default one here. And then I will upload that and Facebook will be happy. So let's just hit confirm. And that's it. So essentially now I have my preferred Ecom setup. So as per the previous training on funnels, I wouldn't necessarily be using all of these

## [03:04 - 04:11]

objectives from the start. But if I was, for example, to set up a new e-commerce store, or if I just wanted to migrate over to the new structure, my main campaigns that I'd be looking at are the page post engagement for cold audiences, the website conversion for cold audiences. I'd probably be playing around with my warm traffic, my hot traffic audiences, and also working with my existing customers as well. So essentially there's no harm switching them all on, but you use them as you need to use them. And also note down here, I've got my dummy account, dummy campaigns, where I can set up ads to share using page post ID. So this has been set up as a website conversion objective because I want to use the features that are available in website conversion on creating my ad. Some of them might be cut out. For example, if I'm pushing into page post engagement, I might not get the same setup in terms of course of action. But that's fine because I'm testing here and when I then move it to my live website conversion campaign, I want all of those features available.
